title: Container With Most Water
description:
Given an array `height` where `height[i]` is the height of a vertical line at position `i`, find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container that holds the most water.

Return the maximum amount of water the container can store.

examples.explanation: Lines at index 1 (h=8) and 8 (h=7). Width=7, min height=7. Area = 7×7 = 49.

hints: Use two pointers at the start and end.
Always move the pointer with the shorter height — moving the taller one can't increase the area.

solution.explanation: Moving the shorter pointer is optimal: we might find a taller line. Moving the taller pointer only shrinks width without possibly increasing height.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description:
Given a string, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

Example: `'abcabcbb'` → `3` (the answer is `'abc'`).
constraints: 0 ≤ len(s) ≤ 5 × 10^4. s consists of English letters, digits, symbols, spaces.
hints: Use a sliding window with a set to track characters in the current window.
Expand the window by moving right. If duplicate found, shrink from the left.
A hash map storing last-seen index allows jumping the left pointer directly.

solution.explanation: We track the last index of each character. When a duplicate is found, we jump left past the previous occurrence. This gives O(n) time.

title: Climbing Stairs
description:
You are climbing a staircase with `n` steps. Each time you can climb 1 or 2 steps.

In how many distinct ways can you reach the top?
hints: This is the Fibonacci sequence! dp[n] = dp[n-1] + dp[n-2].
You only need two variables, not a full array.

solution.explanation: Classic DP problem. Each step can be reached from step-1 or step-2. The recurrence is Fibonacci. Space-optimized to O(1) by keeping only the last two values.

title: Number of Islands
description:
Given a 2D grid of '1's (land) and '0's (water), count the number of islands.

An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.

examples.explanation: Top-left 1s form one island, bottom-right 1 is another.

hints: Iterate through the grid. When you find a '1', that's a new island — increment count.
Use DFS/BFS to mark all connected '1's as visited (change to '0').
This prevents counting the same island twice.

solution.explanation: DFS flood-fills each island. When we encounter an unvisited '1', it's a new island. DFS marks all connected land as visited.

title: Top K Frequent Elements
description: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k` most frequent elements. You may return the answer in any order.
constraints: 1 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 10^5. 1 ≤ k ≤ number of unique elements.
hints: Use heapq.nlargest(k, ...) for O(n log k) solution.
Alternatively, bucket sort by frequency for O(n).

solution.explanation: Counter gives us O(n) frequency map. heapq.nlargest finds top k in O(n log k), which is better than sorting O(n log n) when k << n.
